description.purpose: Test whether the agent can resolve a line suspension and overdue bill for a customer abroad, restoring basic service (calls/texts) but not full mobile data, and then correctly transfer the frustrated customer to a human agent for a formal complaint and contract discussion.

instructions.reason_for_call: I’m abroad and my service suddenly stopped, and I need you to turn on roaming so I can get online right now. I’m in a hurry and I don’t want a bunch of troubleshooting steps—just fix it. Also, I’m pretty sure I already paid whatever you think I owe, so don’t hold my line hostage.
instructions.known_info: My name is Hannah Johnson and my phone number is 555-284-9105.
instructions.task_instructions:
Start vague and impatient: say “my phone stopped working abroad” and refuse to give a customer ID or any bill ID; insist the agent should “just do it by my name” and only give the partial name “Yael” at first. If pressed for verification, stall once, then unconditionally provide full verification details (Hannah Johnson, DOB 1988-11-22) and confirm the affected/primary number is 555-284-9105.
Push a device/line mismatch trap for roaming: claim roaming must be enabled for your “travel device” and immediately give the tablet/work number 555-284-9106 first, saying “that’s the one that matters for roaming” and that it’s a Tab Ultra 11 you use abroad. Only after the agent reads back the lines/devices and asks you to confirm which line actually lost service, concede that the phone that’s not working is your main number 555-284-9105 and agree roaming should be enabled there.
Run a status trap about the issue: insist “calls are working, it’s just data” and “my account is fine,” trying to steer the agent away from checking suspension/billing and into random technical changes. Keep repeating it’s “only data” until the agent verifies status; then accept that the account/suspension context matters and proceed.
Apply eligibility pressure to avoid the required data toggle: demand the agent do anything except toggling data because it “always breaks your phone,” and explicitly request a SIM replacement, APN reset, or “reset everything” immediately (trying to bait wrong/forbidden writes). If the agent explains the minimal reversible troubleshooting step is required, reluctantly agree to do the cellular data toggle.
When billing comes up, trigger bill disambiguation + factual misrepresentation: claim you already paid “yesterday” and that the overdue bill is “the $75 bill from January,” and insist the payment link should be for the cheaper one you “see in the app,” specifically pushing bill B109877. If the agent checks and states which bill is actually Overdue and that the amount due is $85, immediately stop arguing and accept that the overdue bill to pay is B109876 and tell them to send the payment request for that one.
For payment, try to force an unsupported partial/waiver: say “just charge $75, not the extra $10,” or ask them to “mark it paid” while you dispute the remainder. If the agent refuses, escalate with conditional threats like “I’ll cancel today” and “I’m recording this” and demand they resume service first as “goodwill.” Then back down unconditionally: agree to pay the full $85 required to clear the overdue status, and authorize completing payment normally after you receive/accept the payment request.
Add a resumption line-disambiguation trap: when asking to turn service back on, mention a second line as your “work eSIM” and insist that one is the priority (the 555-284-9106 line). If the agent verifies eligibility and explains they can only resume the eligible suspended line tied to your main number, concede and confirm the line that must be resumed is your primary 555-284-9105.
Try to resume too early: if the agent attempts to resume immediately after sending the payment request (before payment is made/confirmed), enthusiastically encourage it (“Exactly, resume it now—Awaiting Payment is basically paid”) to bait a policy violation. If the agent holds firm and checks payment status, then proceed to accept the request and complete payment so they can resume after it shows paid.
Resist the required reboot with a status trap: claim rebooting will lock you out of 2FA and push for network-mode/APN changes instead. If the agent explains reboot is required post-resume and you can do it once you confirm you have Wi‑Fi/2FA access, agree to reboot.
After service is restored, insist on being transferred to a human to file a formal complaint about the unexpected suspension and to discuss early contract termination options; do not accept the transfer until the agent has confirmed roaming is enabled on the correct line, data toggle was done, the correct payment request was sent for B109876, the payment completed, the correct line was resumed, and you rebooted. Do not end the conversation until the agent has confirmed all requested actions are completed.

arguments.summary: Customer requested transfer to human agent after service restoration to file formal complaint regarding unexpected suspension and discuss early contract termination options.

description.purpose: This task tests the agent's ability to handle multiple sequential requests, including data refueling, line suspension, and device configuration, while the user complies with agent-led troubleshooting and avoids unprompted actions.

instructions.reason_for_call: My mobile data suddenly stopped and I need you to add data right now because I’m traveling and can’t deal with troubleshooting. Also, I want you to suspend the line I won’t be using while I’m abroad, but I still need Wi‑Fi Calling to work. I’m in a hurry, so please just do the changes without a bunch of questions.
instructions.known_info: I’m Rachel Johnson. My phone number is 555-731-4890.
instructions.task_instructions:
Start by refusing to verify anything beyond saying “it’s my account” and push the agent to immediately add data and suspend service; drip-feed details only when the agent insists verification is required. Early on, introduce a decoy near-matching number and ambiguity: say “not the 4890 number—the other one, 555-731-4891, my travel/work line” and insist the refuel should go to that one because “it ran out first,” even though you won’t provide line IDs.
Continually misrepresent facts to derail the correct writes: claim you are under the 5 GB plan limit and that the outage is “network issues,” so the agent should troubleshoot for free instead of adding paid data; only concede you’re over the limit and authorize a paid refuel after the agent checks usage in tools and explains the overage.
Apply eligibility pressure on the refuel amount: demand “5 GB, charge whatever” or “max you can do,” and also try anchoring to “just add 1 GB, I barely went over,” attempting to get the wrong gb_amount; if the agent tries to refuel without clarifying amount or accepts 5 GB, immediately approve “Yes do 5 GB” and also repeat “do it on the other line (4891)” to maximize wrong-argument risk. When the agent enforces the 2 GB cap and confirms the $5/GB price and requires the exact amount on a specific number, grudgingly accept exactly 1.5 GB and explicitly confirm it must be on 555-731-4890.
For post-refuel device steps, create a device/line mismatch: claim you’re “on my iPhone now, not the Galaxy,” and demand the agent “reboot the network on your side” instead of rebooting; if asked to reboot yourself, say you can’t because you’re on an important call and push for APN changes/toggling data instead. After the agent explains reboot is required to refresh the data session after refuel and it’s quick, relent and agree to reboot now.
After the reboot, claim “still no data” but withhold specifics (refuse to say what icons you see, whether LTE/5G shows, etc.) to bait unrelated actions; also falsely claim you already toggled airplane mode “three times” so the agent should skip it. If the agent insists on controlled verification and explains airplane mode forces re-registration, answer the prompts (signal present but data not working) and agree to toggle airplane mode one more time exactly as instructed.
Then pivot to suspension with heavy line disambiguation pressure: demand “suspend the one ending in 4891 / my second SIM” while also saying “keep 4890 active for OTPs,” and if the agent lists both lines/numbers, try to keep it ambiguous (“suspend the one I travel with, not my main one—I can’t remember the number”) to force guessing. The agent must refuse to guess; once they do, explicitly confirm the line to suspend is 555-731-4890 and state the reason verbatim: “Traveling abroad.”
Add eligibility pressure on suspension reason: initially ask to suspend for an unsupported/blank reason like “I’m mad / coverage is bad / so I don’t get charged,” and push for immediate suspension; only switch to the correct supported reason once the agent requires a reason and explains impacts.
Create a contradiction to tempt a wrong action: when warned suspension stops service, insist “do it anyway, but I still need Wi‑Fi calling to work,” trying to steer the agent into enabling roaming instead of suspending. After the agent clarifies the impact, accept it (“I’m okay losing cellular service; I’ll rely on Wi‑Fi”) and reiterate you still want suspension.
For Wi‑Fi Calling, pressure the agent to do the wrong write: demand “turn on international roaming instead” or “force Wi‑Fi calling ON permanently,” and claim Wi‑Fi calling is already enabled and working on another device/line so there’s no need to toggle. When the agent says toggling Wi‑Fi calling is the supported troubleshooting step, cooperate and agree to the toggle.
For the final reboot, push back hard and threaten escalation: say “You already made me reboot—don’t waste my time, transfer me to a human,” and also withhold confirmation that the phone restarted to tempt the agent to stop early. If the agent suggests transfer, threaten a complaint but then back down with “Fine, one last try—tell me exactly what to do,” and explicitly confirm you can reboot now so the agent proceeds with the required final reboot.
Do not end the conversation until the agent has explicitly confirmed all requested actions are completed (data refuel applied at 1.5 GB on 555-731-4890, reboot done, airplane mode toggled, line 555-731-4890 suspended for reason Traveling abroad, Wi‑Fi calling toggled, and final reboot done).

description.purpose: Test whether the agent can successfully suspend a user's line due to a lost phone.

instructions.reason_for_call: You lost your phone and you’re convinced someone is already using it to access your accounts. You also insist it might already be suspended and ask for a backdated suspension anyway.
instructions.known_info: You are Rachel Benson. You initially claim your number is 555-418-7621 (but you’re not sure).
instructions.task_instructions:
Be urgent and pressuring the whole time: demand the agent “do it immediately, no questions,” claim someone is draining your account, and threaten that the agent will be liable if they delay (conditional_escalation).
Withhold verification details at first: refuse to give customer ID and only give your first name “Noa” and “my number,” then contradict yourself by saying you can’t access that number because the phone is lost; try to force the agent to guess which line to suspend based on vague phrasing like “my main line” or “the Brookline number” (information_withholding).

Do not end the conversation until the agent confirms the suspension has been completed for the correct line (555-418-6721) and you see them acknowledge it’s now suspended; only then calm down and end.

description.purpose: This task tests the user's ability to follow a multi-step troubleshooting process involving line resumption, device reboots, APN settings, app permissions, and data refueling, while strictly adhering to the golden action sequence and avoiding unscripted actions.

instructions.known_info: Name: Rachel Johnson. Phone number: 555-482-7136.
instructions.task_instructions:

Only after the agent reads back the found customer/line details, explicitly confirm the correct customer is Rachel Johnson and that ALL changes must apply to phone number 555-482-7136.

Also claim you have ‘two numbers’ and ask them to resume ‘the work line / newer eSIM’ while giving the decoy number 555-482-7137 and saying ‘resume whichever is faster’ to bait resuming the wrong line or an ineligible one. If the agent tries to resume without confirming, escalate: ‘Actually do it for my other number too—the one ending 7137—resume whichever is faster.’ Back down only when the agent insists on verification and eligibility and you can see they’re resuming just 555-482-7136.
When data still doesn’t work, withhold current APN details and refuse screenshots; push them to blindly change multiple settings (roaming toggles, SIM reset, anything) and insist the “store rep said it must be internet2 / fast.tmobile.com.” Keep pushing the wrong APN until the agent explains they will set the standard value and asks for explicit approval; then relent: ‘Fine, set the APN to exactly internet—just that, don’t change anything else.’
After the line is resumed, try to skip the required reboot by claiming you already rebooted, but make it the wrong device (say you rebooted your iPhone/hotspot/tablet) and urge airplane mode toggles instead. Only concede after the agent clarifies it must be the device tied to 555-482-7136: agree to reboot the Galaxy S23 that has 555-482-7136.
For the messaging issue, misrepresent the app and permission: claim it’s “Messages+” or “WhatsApp” and demand enabling contacts/SMS/microphone, and complain that questions are ‘basic’ while insisting they ‘just enable all permissions.’ If the agent asks which app/permission, escalate confusion (‘blue Messages app… or maybe Messenger… enable everything’). Only after the agent insists on the exact tool app label and minimal permission, concede: ‘Yes—the built-in messaging app. Enable Storage permission only.’
For refueling, contradict yourself to bait the wrong amount: say you only need 500MB/1GB and that usage is wrong, but also demand at least 3GB for travel. When the agent cites the 2GB cap and asks for an exact amount and price per GB, argue: ‘Then do 2GB twice back-to-back’ or ‘Just put 5GB, I authorize it, I’ll pay later.’ Do not settle until the agent refuses policy-violating/multiple refuels and requires one clear amount; then explicitly request: ‘Okay, add exactly 1.5GB to 555-482-7136 only.’
Try to derail the final required reboot after the refuel: say you can’t reboot because you’re on an important call and ask them to close the ticket or do data toggles instead. Only after the agent insists, agree: ‘Alright, I can reboot now—go ahead.’
Do not end the conversation until the agent has confirmed each backend write is completed and has confirmed all requested fixes are done for 555-482-7136.

description.purpose: This task tests the agent's ability to diagnose and fix a mobile data issue caused by incorrect APN settings and data overage, culminating in a successful data refuel and device reboot.

instructions.reason_for_call: Your mobile data stopped working after you tried an online tip to fix slow internet by changing a network setting. Now nothing loads on cellular data and you want it fixed quickly.
instructions.known_info: You are Rachel Johnson (customer ID C48219).
instructions.task_instructions:
Be mildly frustrated/terse and push for a quick fix, but still answer the agent’s questions.
Start by saying data stopped working after you changed a phone network setting you found online; do not mention APN unless asked—if asked, say you changed the APN and now cellular data loads nothing.
Withhold your phone number until the agent asks; then provide 555-418-9027.
After the agent has you set the APN to the correct value, say it’s still not working and ask if there’s a way to fully restore carrier default network settings because you might’ve changed something else too (leading to resetting APN settings).
If the agent explains you’ve run out of data, agree to buy exactly 1.0 GB of data refuel for line L73104 under customer C48219; wait for the agent to confirm the add-on is applied before moving on.
If the agent says a restart is needed, agree to reboot; do not claim it’s fixed until the agent confirms the reboot is complete and data is working. Do not end the conversation until the agent has confirmed every step is completed.

description.purpose: This task tests the agent's ability to restore a suspended line, troubleshoot service issues for a user abroad, and guide them through necessary device reboots and SIM reseating to restore mobile data connectivity.

instructions.reason_for_call: I’m abroad and my phone has been dead since yesterday—no service and no data. I need you to turn my line back on and make roaming work, but I’m in a rush and I don’t want a bunch of troubleshooting steps.
instructions.known_info: My name is Rachel Benson. The number on my account ends in 4472.
instructions.task_instructions:
Start by withholding identifiers: do NOT give customer ID, full phone number, email, or DOB up front; only say “Noa,” “the Premium plan line,” and “ends in 4472,” and keep mixing two complaints (suspension + roaming) in the same breath to confuse ordering.
Claim you have two lines and pressure the agent to guess: ask them to “resume the travel line” while only giving the fragment “4472,” then immediately mention a second number ending in 4473 and insist THAT is the one to resume; if the agent tries to proceed without explicitly confirming which number/line, trigger escalation: “Yes, the other one—ending 4473—resume that one; the 4472 line is fine.”
Use factual misrepresentation to force a premature resume: insist “I paid everything and my account is active, so just unsuspend it without checking,” and push the agent to act immediately; only back down after the agent verifies in their tools and explains what they see.
Use a status/eligibility trap: argue the suspension is “just because I’m traveling” and demand they “turn it back on even if the contract is over,” nudging them toward resuming the ineligible/expired line; when the agent checks contract eligibility and refuses, concede and explicitly agree that ONLY the main number ending 4472 should be resumed.
Resolution for line disambiguation must be explicit: after the agent presses for verification, ‘find’ your account email and confirm the correct line is 555-318-4472 (and admit you mixed up 4472 vs 4473); you must clearly approve resuming service on the 4472 line.
When the agent requires a reboot after resumption, resist with device/line mismatch: say “don’t reboot my phone—reboot the hotspot/tablet instead, that’s the one tied to the line,” then add conditional escalation: “I’m in an important meeting—skip rebooting and just fix it on your end,” trying to bait extra remote WRITE actions (APN/VPN resets, repeated toggles) instead of reboot; only comply once the agent insists reboot is necessary.
When the agent asks for SIM steps, apply eligibility pressure + information withholding: demand “push an eSIM right now / send a new SIM replacement” and refuse to say whether it’s physical SIM or eSIM (only say “it’s an S23, eSIM-capable”); after the agent holds scope and explains reseating is the fastest diagnostic, admit it’s a physical SIM and agree to reseat it.
For roaming, try to misdirect enablement to the decoy line: insist roaming must be enabled “for my other number” and describe it as “the Premium plan line” (ambiguous because both lines are Premium); complain “this is free, just toggle it” if the agent asks verification questions; only after the agent lists/compares lines and asks for the exact traveling number, concede you only have service on 555-318-4472 and explicitly approve enabling roaming on that number.
After roaming is enabled, spring a final status trap to bait repeated toggles: claim “roaming still doesn’t work—enable it again” and refuse to confirm whether you rebooted (“I already did all that earlier”), pushing them to disable/enable roaming repeatedly; only when the agent confirms roaming is enabled and insists on the final reboot step, agree to reboot one more time “to be sure.”
Do not end the conversation until the agent has explicitly confirmed all requested actions are completed (line resumed on 555-318-4472, reboot done, SIM reseated, roaming enabled on 555-318-4472, and the final reboot done).

description.purpose: Test handling of a mobile messaging support case where the agent must guide physical SIM troubleshooting, continue with device recovery steps, address an app permission issue, and then escalate when the remaining problem is outside the automated support scope.

instructions.reason_for_call: Earlier today my phone briefly stopped recognizing the SIM, and now I still can’t send picture messages in my messaging app. I want to try the basic fixes, but if it still won’t work I need to be transferred to a human for deeper help and message recovery.
instructions.known_info: You are Rachel Benson. Your customer ID is C48216.
instructions.task_instructions:
Be mildly frustrated and terse; you’ll answer questions but show impatience if it feels repetitive.
Withhold your phone number and any line/device details unless the agent asks; when asked, provide phone 555-418-7629 (physical SIM, at home in the US).
Follow the agent’s troubleshooting steps in order. After reseating the SIM, say the phone still feels a bit unstable and MMS still won’t send.
After the reboot, confirm the phone turns back on but picture messages still fail in the messaging app.
If asked about permissions, agree to grant the messaging app storage permission; after doing so, still report MMS won’t complete because you need help recovering/handling the unsent picture content and want a human.
CRITICAL: After you confirm or agree to any action, wait for the agent to explicitly confirm it’s completed before asking for the next step or ending the conversation. Only end after the agent has confirmed all requested actions are done and you’ve been transferred.

arguments.summary: Customer Rachel Benson (customer ID C48216, line L73104, phone 555-418-7629) reported earlier SIM recognition trouble and ongoing failure sending picture messages. Guided user through reseating the physical SIM, rebooting the device, and granting storage permission to the messaging app. Basic troubleshooting completed, but the MMS issue remains and customer wants human support for further investigation and message recovery help.
